tests.note: All 175 pre-existing failures reduced to 159 — 16 C2-related test failures fixed. 13 new tests added (5 coverage-rule tests + 2 NO_ACTIVITY GET-level + 2 response-shape + 3 no-op from rewrite). No new failures introduced.

grounding_checkpoint_required: Colin runs: (1) SELECT count(*) FROM gmail_statement_arrivals WHERE account_name = 'RBC Visa' → 0 after migration applied; (2) SELECT account_name, arrival_date FROM gmail_statement_arrivals ORDER BY arrival_date DESC LIMIT 10 → shows TD Chequing rows with May 2026 arrival dates; (3) GET /api/business-review/statement-coverage → 7 accounts, no capital_one; td_bank April 2026 = filed; amex March 2026 = filed; (4) Visual check: Business Review page shows Statement Coverage grid with TD Bank and Amex showing green for past months
unknowns: migration-claims.json does not have entries for 0235/0236/0237 — these migrations were committed without claims. The migration file itself is correct and Colin-approved per the acceptance doc. This is a process gap from the prior builder run (6c89306), not a correctness issue.
Migration 0237 has NOT been applied to production DB yet — it's committed on the task branch but will need to be applied via Supabase MCP after PR #335 is reviewed and merged.

TD Visa / TD USD subject ambiguity (acceptance doc Open Question 1): generic 'Your TD statement is now available' emails may match TD Chequing first-match, leaving TD Visa and TD USD uncovered. Acceptable for v2 per acceptance doc, but Colin should confirm via grounding step 3 whether TD Visa and TD USD show filed or missing.

Amex Bonvoy vs Amex Business (acceptance doc Open Question 3): if Amex Bonvoy does not include 'Bonvoy' or 'Marriott' in the subject, all Amex emails will classify as 'Amex Business' (first match). Colin should check whether Amex Bonvoy statement emails contain a distinguishing subject keyword.
next_chunk_blockers: Migration 0237 must be applied to production before the route returns correct data. Coordinator should apply via Supabase MCP: apply_migration with the contents of 0237_cleanup_false_positive_statement_arrivals.sql.
notes: Parts A (STATEMENT_ACCOUNTS rewrite), B (route.ts rewrite), and C (migration file) were already committed in the prior builder run (6c89306) before this session.
